name: NXMD (EMS)
mission: To preserve life using in-depth roleplay, elevated realism, and professional medical conduct.
vision: A crucial pillar of server functionality where creativity forms the standard for treatment, and scene safety dictates operation.

rules.title: Absence Notification Policy
points: Inactivity of 5+ days must be reported to a Senior Officer or filed through an official Leave Request.
Unexplained inactivity can lead to immediate removal from the department.
Extended leave (>14 days) requires explicit authorization from the EMS Chief or Senior Administration.

rules.title: Radio Discipline & Communication
points: Report on-duty with: 'EMS active, 10-8.'
Keep transmissions brief and mission-relevant (calls, patient info, status updates).
Stay active on radio unless: Off-duty, In Training, Performing Surgeries, or Confidential Discussions.
Excessive chatter, non-RP talk, or joking on the radio is strictly prohibited.

rules.title: Scene Safety Guidelines
points: No Entry: Do not intervene in dangerous areas (shootouts, robberies, gang conflicts) without LEO support.
Always request police support for prison or crime-scene calls.
If no LEOs are available, do not proceed — mark the call as 'unreachable' for safety.

rules.title: Treatment & Transport Protocols
points: In-depth Roleplay: Perform realistic medical assessments for all injuries.
Creativity: Enhanced procedures (CPR, splinting, field surgery) are highly encouraged.
Consent: Always required unless the patient is unconscious or severely impaired.
Transports: Update radio with '10-80, en-route to hospital with [X] patient'.

rules.title: Promotion Pathway
points: Factors: Consistent presence, professionalism, positive attitude, and creative medical RP.
Disqualifiers: Repeatedly requesting promotions, active infractions, or poor conduct.
Earn recognition through professional actions, not words alone.

rules.title: Expected Conduct
points: Maintain professional IC behavior at all times, especially while at HQ.
Stock medical supplies regularly to ensure constant readiness.
De-escalate confrontations or report issues after a scene has concluded.
Never carry non-medical weapons or contraband on duty. Corruption results in immediate removal.

rules.title: Vehicle Usage Rules
points: Only drive assigned EMS units. Personal/civilian vehicle use is prohibited on duty.
Practice safe, realistic driving. Reckless maneuvers are forbidden.
Use emergency signals (lights/sirens) only when medically justified.

rules.title: Immersive Medical RP Standards
points: Roleplay every action: Even basics like applying bandages or taking vitals.
Avoid robotic /me commands. Tailor your responses to each patient situation.
Avoid 'revive and go' - always leave a lasting, realistic roleplay impression.

rules.title: Hospital Etiquette
points: Stay alert at HQ. Do not loiter in lobbies or clutter the ER.
Maintain character and avoid OOC talk while in uniform.
When in doubt, consult a superior through appropriate IC/OOC channels.

rules.title: Blacklist Offenses
points: Abusing the medic role to assist in criminal acts.
Reviving teammates during active gunfights or hostile situations.
Failing to follow department SOPs or treating EMS as a non-RP job.

rules.title: Forbidden RP Scenarios
points: No Revivals: Burnt, exploded, or severely mangled bodies.
No Revivals: Deep-sea drownings or unrecoverable bodies.
No Collusion: Working as a scout, mole, or spy for criminal organizations.

roles.name: Medical Director
roles.desc: Head of the Nexus Medical Department — Principal Overseer of all administrative and medical operations.

roles.name: Dean
roles.desc: Administration — High-level strategist responsible for department-wide policy and medical excellence.

roles.name: Assistant Dean
roles.desc: Administration — Operational lead managing day-to-day department logistics and hospital flow.

roles.name: Neurosurgeon (NSG)
roles.desc: Surgical Tier — Highest level of operative precision for critical neurological trauma.

roles.name: Orthopedic Surgeon (ORT)
roles.desc: Surgical Tier — Expert in skeletal reconstruction and complex bone trauma management.

roles.name: Anesthesiologist (ANE)
roles.desc: Specialist Tier — Managing critical care sedation and intraoperative patient stability.

roles.name: Pathologist (PAT)
roles.desc: Specialist Tier — Leading clinical research, laboratory forensics, and diagnostic analysis.

roles.name: Neurologist (NEU)
roles.desc: Expert diagnostics focused on advanced brain and central nervous system pathology.

roles.name: Psychiatrist (PSY)
roles.desc: Provider of crucial mental health evaluations and psychiatric medicine for high-stakes roles.

roles.name: ENT Specialist (ENT)
roles.desc: Otolaryngology lead for specialized ear, nose, and throat diagnostic procedures.

roles.name: Pediatrician (PED)
roles.desc: Primary caregiver for the neonatal and younger demographic within Nexus City.

roles.name: Dermatologist (DER)
roles.desc: Specialist in soft tissue management, grafts, and advanced skin trauma diagnostics.

roles.name: General Physician (GPH)
roles.desc: Allied Health — Essential comprehensive primary care and specialized triage lead.

roles.name: Diagnostic Specialist (RAD)
roles.desc: Allied Health — Primary operator of radiological imaging and diagnostic equipment.

roles.name: ER Technician (ERT)
roles.desc: Allied Health — Specialist in hospital intake, emergency stabilization, and rapid triage.

roles.name: Physiotherapist (PHY)
roles.desc: Allied Health — Guiding survivors through rigorous post-trauma physical rehabilitation.

roles.name: Senior Paramedic (SPM)
roles.desc: Staff Tier — Distinguished field supervisor and mentor to the probationary response team.

roles.name: Paramedic (PAR)
roles.desc: Staff Tier — Fully qualified emergency responder and frontline medical officer.

roles.name: Junior Paramedic (JPM)
roles.desc: Staff Tier — Tactical responder undergoing advanced field certification.

roles.name: Intern (INT)
roles.desc: Staff Tier — Probationary cadet undergoing rigorous clinical observation and field training.
